1267	/-- **(iii-1) Unit expansion: inverting the comp-coherence.** For composable `a : X₁ ⟶ X₂`,
1268	`b : X₂ ⟶ X₃`, a module `N` on `X₃`, and `g' = a ≫ b`, the bare `(g')`-unit expands as the
1269	four-factor composite `η^b ≫ b_*(η^a) ≫ pushforwardComp(a,b).hom ≫ g'_*(pullbackComp(a,b).hom)`.
1270	This is the inversion of the leg-reindex engine `pullbackPushforward_unit_comp`: post-composing its
1271	identity with `g'_*(pullbackComp.hom)` collapses the trailing `pullbackComp.inv` (functoriality of
1272	`g'_*` on `inv ≫ hom = id`). See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand`. -/
1273	theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}}
1274	    (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃) (N : X₃.Modules) :
1275	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N
1276	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N ≫
1277	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1278	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
1279	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)) ≫
1280	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app
1281	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))) ≫
1282	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
1283	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N) := by
1284	  rw [pullbackPushforward_unit_comp a b N]
1285	  -- The two trailing `(a ≫ b)_*`-images cancel (`pullbackComp.inv ≫ pullbackComp.hom = 𝟙`), but a
1286	  -- spurious `X.Modules` instance diamond under single-file elaboration makes `rw [Category.assoc]`
1287	  -- / `rw [hI]` / `simp only [Iso.inv_hom_id_app]` misfire on the goal's locked composition. We
1288	  -- prove the cancellation on FRESH terms (where the rewrites match) and discharge the goal in
1289	  -- term mode (`exact`), which checks defeq and tolerates the diamond.
1290	  have hI : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv.app N) ≫
1291	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N)
1292	      = 𝟙 _ := by
1293	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
1294	  have hfin := (congrArg (fun z =>
1295	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N ≫ z) hI).trans
1296	    (Category.comp_id _)
1297	  exact ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans hfin).symm

1302	is `(Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ_R`, giving the four `Γ`-image factors of the blueprint. See blueprint
1303	`lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute`. -/

1307	    F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N)

1310	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app

1829	
1830	The argument is carried out directly on the module of global sections; no abstract adjoint-mate
1831	identification at the level of sheaves intervenes. By `ModuleCat` hom-extensionality it suffices to
1832	check on the `R'`-module generators `1 ⊗ₜ x` (`ext x`), leaving the concrete per-generator identity
1833	`(Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(θ) ≫ Θ_tgt) (1 ⊗ x) = regroupEquiv.inv (1 ⊗ x)`. By construction `Γ(θ)` is, read on
1834	global sections through the two tilde dictionaries `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` /
1835	`pullback_spec_tilde_iso` packaged in `Θ_src`, `Θ_tgt`, the extension of scalars along `ψ : R → R'`
1836	of the algebraic unit of extension of scalars along `A → A ⊗_R R'` (`m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`); evaluating
1837	through the `R'`-action on the target gives `1 ⊗ x ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ x`, which is exactly
1838	`regroupEquiv.inv` on generators (both sides `R'`-linear). This theorem's **body has no inline
1839	`sorry`** (it unfolds `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` to its `(g^* ⊣ g_*)`-counit form
1840	(`Adjunction.homEquiv_counit`) and is then exactly Seam 3, `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose ψ φ M`),
1841	but it is **transitively `sorry`-backed** through `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`. The single
1842	residual obligation
1843	of the whole Seam-3 chain — the mate-unwinding coherence over the generic pullback square — lives in
1844	`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (and, beneath it, in the Seam-2 leg-reindex
1845	`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs`), NOT here. With that discharged,
1846	`base_change_mate_generator_trace` is a one-line corollary. -/
1847	theorem base_change_mate_section_identity {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
1848	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1849	    (base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
1850	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1851	        (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
1852	          (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1853	          (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1854	          (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) ≫
1855	      (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom
1856	      = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv := by
1857	  -- COUNIT FACTORIZATION (blueprint Seam 3 step): `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is by definition the
1858	  -- `(g^* ⊣ g_*)`-adjunction transpose `((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm inner`
1859	  -- of the inner pushforward comparison `inner`. By `Adjunction.homEquiv_counit` this transpose is

/-- **(iii, links 1+3 fused) Distribute the inner `(a ≫ b)`-unit through `(Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ_R` and
collapse the transparent `pushforwardComp` middle factor.** On CLEAN terms (a single instance of the
`X.Modules` category struct is in play, so keyed `rw` fires, unlike on the locked main goal), the
`Γ_R`-image of the `(Spec φ)_*`-image of the `(a ≫ b)`-pullback–pushforward unit at `N` distributes,
via `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute`, into four `Γ_R∘(Spec φ)_*`-image factors,
of which the third (the `pushforwardComp a b` hom-coherence) collapses to `𝟙` by
`base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp`. The result is the three-factor reduced form
`Γ(G η^b) ≫ Γ(G (b_* η^a)) ≫ Γ(G ((a≫b)_* (pullbackComp a b).hom))`. This is the diamond-free
building block to be spliced (via `congrArg`/`.trans`, then a defeq-bridging `exact`) into the locked
main goal of `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs`. -/
theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_distributeCollapse {X₁ X₂ : Scheme.{u}}
    {R A : CommRingCat.{u}} (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ Spec A) (φ : R ⟶ A) (N : X₂.Modules) :
    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).map
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N))
      = (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N))
        ≫ (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).map
              ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
                ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
                  ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))))
        ≫ (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).map
              ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
                ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N))) := by
  rw [base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute a b N
    (F := Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R))]

  rw [base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp a b φ
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)),

    {R A : CommRingCat.{u}} (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ Spec A) (φ : R ⟶ A) (N : (Spec A).Modules) :

    {R A : CommRingCat.{u}} (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ Spec A) (φ : R ⟶ A) (N : (Spec A).Modules) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N)
      = (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N)
        ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)))
        ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N)) := by
  -- Distribute `(Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ_R` over the four `unitExpand` factors via `gammaDistribute` at the
  -- composite functor `F := (Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ_R`. Everything stays in the single `F.map` form
  -- (no `Functor.comp_map` re-elaboration), so the third factor `F.map ((pushforwardComp a b).hom)`
  -- and the `gammaDistribute` output carry the SAME `F` instance — keyed `rw` fires (no diamond).
  -- Factor 3 (the `pushforwardComp a b` hom-coherence) has identity `F`-image: its section value
  -- is `𝟙`, and `F` carries identities to identities.
  have hFc : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))) = 𝟙 _ :=
    (congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
      (show (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)) = 𝟙 _ from rfl)).trans
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map_id _)
  rw [base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute a b N
    (F := Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)), hFc,
    Category.comp_id, Category.assoc]

## Blueprint dependency graph (leandag)

You can navigate the project's dependency graph read-only to find the
proven lemmas/defs your goal can lean on (with the exact `\lean{}`
names to apply) instead of re-deriving them. `archon` is on PATH;
`--json` prints parseable JSON to stdout (banner to stderr):

```
archon dag-query ancestors --node <blueprint-label-of-your-target>   # proven deps available to you
archon dag-query node      --node <label>                            # one declaration's status
```

It is a navigation aid only — the proof obligation and your blueprint
chapter remain the source of truth.

Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean

```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).
57	
58	## Mathlib status
59	
60	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
61	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
62	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
63	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
64	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
65	- `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
66	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
67	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
68	
69	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
70	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
71	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
72	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
73	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
74	
75	## References
76	
77	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`. Source:
78	Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", §1 (FGA Explained
79	Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–35); cf. Hartshorne III.5.2.
80	-/
81	
82	set_option autoImplicit false
83	
84	universe u
85	
86	open CategoryTheory Limits
87	
88	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
89	
90	namespace Scheme
91	
92	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
93	
94	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
95	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
96	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
97	polynomial is the function
98	
99	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
100	
101	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
102	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
105	III.5.2). -/
106	
107	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
108	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
109	
110	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
111	
112	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
113	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
114	
115	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
116	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
117	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
118	
119	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
120	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
121	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
122	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
123	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
124	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
125	    Polynomial ℚ :=
126	  sorry
127	
128	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
129	
130	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
131	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
132	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
133	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
134	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
135	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
136	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
137	
138	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
139	
140	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
141	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
142	
143	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
144	
145	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
146	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
147	
148	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
149	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
150	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
151	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
152	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
153	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
154	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
155	
156	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
157	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
158	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
159	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
160	is a typed `sorry`. -/
161	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
162	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
163	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
164	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
165	  sorry
166	
167	end Scheme
168	
169	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
170	
171	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
172	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
173	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
174	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
175	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
176	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
177	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
178	
179	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
180	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
181	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
182	
183	namespace Scheme
184	
185	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
186	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
187	
188	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
189	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
190	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
191	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
192	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
193	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
194	
195	iter-177+: the body re-exports `QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) (?) V Φ_d`, where
196	`Φ_d : Polynomial ℚ` is the constant polynomial `d`. For the iter-176
197	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
198	noncomputable def Grassmannian {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
199	    (_V : S.Modules) (_d : ℕ) :
200	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
201	  sorry
202	
203	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
204	
205	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
206	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
207	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
208	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
209	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
210	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
211	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
212	
213	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
214	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
215	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
216	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
217	work (once the proof body lands).
218	
219	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
220	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
221	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
222	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
223	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
224	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
225	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
226	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
227	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
228	  sorry
229	
230	end Scheme
231	
232	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Quot/Grassmannian predicates
233	
234	These declarations build the support/freeness predicates of
235	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`, §"Support and freeness
236	predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no rank-`d` local
237	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme, so it is built here. -/
238	
239	namespace SheafOfModules
240	
241	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
242	
243	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
244	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
245	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
246	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
247	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
248	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).
249	
250	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
251	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
252	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
253	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
254	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
255	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
256	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
257	
258	end SheafOfModules
259	
260	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator ideal sheaf and schematic support
261	
262	These declarations build the annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules and the
263	support/properness predicates of `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`,
264	§"Support and freeness predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no
265	annihilator ideal sheaf for sheaves of modules on a scheme, nor a schematic-support
266	or proper-support predicate, so they are built here.
267	
268	The annihilator is packaged via `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, exactly mirroring
269	Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
270	`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
271	family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
272	internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
273	makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
274	`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
275	which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
276	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
277	the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
278	(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
279	`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
280	
281	namespace Scheme.Modules
282	
283	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
284	
285	/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
286	(`def:modules_annihilator`).
287	
288	On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
289	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
290	is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
291	in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
292	sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
293	the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
294	characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).
295	
296	This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
297	for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
298	noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
299	  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)
300	
301	/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
302	module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
303	of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
304	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
305	lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
306	    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
307	  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
308	
309	/-- The **schematic support** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
310	(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
311	ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
312	noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
313	  (annihilator F).subscheme
314	
315	/-- The canonical closed immersion of the schematic support into the ambient scheme,
316	realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
317	This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
318	`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
319	`annihilator` is. -/
320	noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
321	  (annihilator F).subschemeι
322	
323	/-- The sheaf of modules `F` **has proper support over `S` along `f`**
324	(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with
325	`f : X ⟶ S` is a proper morphism. Since `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` is stable under
326	base change, this condition is preserved by pullback, as required by the Quot functor's
327	pullback action. Project-local because `schematicSupport` is. -/
328	def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
329	  IsProper (schematicSupportι F ≫ f)
330	
331	end Scheme.Modules
332	
333	end AlgebraicGeometry
334	
335	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
336	
337	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
338	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
339	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
340	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
341	annihilator commutes with localization:
342	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
343	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
344	-/
345	
346	namespace Module
347	
348	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
349	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
350	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
351	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
352	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
353	
354	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
355	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
356	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
357	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
358	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
359	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
360	
361	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
362	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
363	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
364	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
365	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
366	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
367	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
368	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
369	  classical
370	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
371	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
372	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
373	    intro r h
374	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
375	    intro x
376	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
377	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
378	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
379	    | zero => simp
380	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
381	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
382	  apply le_antisymm
383	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
384	    intro y hy
385	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
386	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
387	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
388	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
389	      intro m hm
390	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
391	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
392	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
393	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
394	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
395	    choose u hu using hgen
396	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
397	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
398	      intro m hm
399	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
400	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)
401	      have hcoe : ((∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2 : S) : R) = (u m hm : R) * (c : R) := by
402	        rw [hc]; push_cast; ring
403	      rw [hcoe, mul_smul, smul_comm, hu m hm, smul_zero]
404	    have hUa : (U : R) * a ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
405	      apply key; intro m hm; rw [mul_smul]; exact hU m hm
406	    have heq : IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ a s
407	        = (algebraMap R Rₚ ((U : R) * a)) * IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ 1 (U * s) := by
408	      rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_eq_mul_mk'_one, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq]
409	      push_cast; ring
410	    rw [heq]
411	    exact Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hUa)
412	  · -- `(Ann R M).map ⊆ Ann Rₚ Mₚ`: the image of an annihilator annihilates
413	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
414	    intro a ha
415	    rw [Ideal.mem_comap, Module.mem_annihilator]
416	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at ha
417	    intro x
418	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f x
419	    dsimp only [Function.uncurry]
420	    rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_one (M := S) Rₚ a, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, ha m,
421	      IsLocalizedModule.mk'_zero]
422	
423	end Module
424	
425	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections localize on a basic open
426	
427	This section builds, bottom-up, toward the keystone
428	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`: for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M`
429	on a scheme `X`, an affine open `U`, and `f ∈ Γ(X,U)`, the restriction
430	`M(U) → M(D(f))` exhibits `M(D(f))` as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X,U)`.
431	
432	The substance is the affine (Spec-local) computation: over `Spec R`, a quasi-coherent
433	sheaf is `Ñ = tilde N` for `N = Γ(M)`, and the basic-open restriction of `Ñ` is the
434	module localization map. Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` namespace already carries
435	the localization fact for `tilde N` *as the map out of `N`* (the instance
436	`IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen N (basicOpen f)).hom`). The first building
437	block below repackages this as a statement about the *presheaf restriction map* of `Ñ`
438	itself (from global sections to `D(f)`), which is the form the downstream scheme-level
439	argument consumes after the affine identification `M|_U ≅ Ñ`.
440	
441	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does **not** prove that an arbitrary quasi-coherent sheaf
442	on `Spec R` lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the comment in
443	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean` says this "will later be shown"); the
444	equivalence `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` is a genuine gap. Consequently the keystone for an
445	*arbitrary* quasi-coherent `M` is gated on that bridge (`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso
446	M.fromTildeΓ`); the building blocks here are stated for `tilde N` directly, and for a
447	general `M : (Spec R).Modules` under the explicit hypothesis `[IsIso M.fromTildeΓ]`
448	(equivalently, `M` in the essential image of `tilde`). -/
449	
450	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
451	
452	open CategoryTheory Limits
453	
454	/-- **Basic-open restriction of a `tilde` sheaf is a module localization.**
455	
456	For `N : ModuleCat R` and `f : R`, the presheaf restriction map of the associated sheaf
457	`Ñ = tilde N` from global sections `Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(Ñ, D(f))` exhibits the
458	latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
459	
460	This is the affine, Spec-local heart of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. Mathlib
461	carries the localization fact for the map `tilde.toOpen N (D f) : N → Γ(Ñ, D(f))` out of
462	`N`; since `tilde.toOpen N ⊤ : N → Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is an isomorphism and
463	`tilde.toOpen N (D f) = tilde.toOpen N ⊤ ≫ restriction` (`tilde.toOpen_res`), precomposing
464	the localization map with the inverse isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`)
465	transfers the property to the restriction map. Project-local: Mathlib states the fact only
466	for the map out of `N`, not for the presheaf restriction of `Ñ`. -/
467	theorem isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
468	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
469	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
470	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
471	  set res := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
472	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op with hresdef
473	  have hres := tilde.toOpen_res N ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
474	    (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤))
475	  -- `e : N ≃ₗ Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is the global-sections isomorphism of the tilde sheaf.
476	  set e : N ≃ₗ[R] _ := (tilde.isoTop N).toLinearEquiv with hedef
477	  have key : (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom = res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap := by
478	    rw [hedef, ← hres]; rfl
479	  have hinst0 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
480	      (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom := inferInstance
481	  rw [key] at hinst0
482	  set g := res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap with hg
483	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) g := hinst0
484	  have h2 := IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := g) e.symm
485	  have he : g ∘ₗ e.symm.toLinearMap = res.hom := by
486	    apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
487	    change res.hom (e (e.symm x)) = res.hom x
488	    rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
489	  rw [he] at h2
490	  exact h2
491	
492	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a sheaf in the essential image of `tilde`.**
493	
494	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` whose tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an
495	isomorphism (equivalently, `M` lies in the essential image of the `tilde` functor — the
496	honest Spec-affine stand-in for quasi-coherence, see the section header), the presheaf
497	restriction map of `M` from global sections `Γ(M, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(M, D(f))`
498	exhibits the latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
499	
500	This transports `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` across the isomorphism
501	`M.fromTildeΓ : tilde N ⟶ M` (where `N = Γ(M, ⊤)`): the induced presheaf isomorphism is
502	natural in the open, so on each of `⊤` and `D(f)` it provides an `R`-linear isomorphism
503	intertwining the two restriction maps. Post- and pre-composing the localization map for
504	`tilde N` with these isomorphisms (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`,
505	`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`) yields the property for `M`.
506	
507	Project-local: it is the affine engine of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. The
508	general quasi-coherent case additionally requires the (currently Mathlib-absent) bridge
509	`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. -/
510	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
511	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso M.fromTildeΓ] (f : R) :
512	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
513	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
514	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
515	  -- the presheaf-level isomorphism induced by the (iso) counit `M.fromTildeΓ`
516	  let ψ := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget (ModuleCat R) (Spec R)).map (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ)
517	  haveI : IsIso ψ := inferInstance
518	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens))) := inferInstance
519	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))) := inferInstance
520	  -- the component isomorphisms as `R`-linear equivalences
521	  let eTop : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)))).toLinearEquiv
522	  let eDf : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))).toLinearEquiv
523	  -- the restriction map of `tilde N` (localizes by `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict`)
524	  let rt := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj
525	        (tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)))).presheaf.map
526	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
527	  -- naturality square of `ψ` for `D(f) ⟶ ⊤`
528	  have hnat := ψ.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op
529	  have hnathom := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hnat
530	  rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp] at hnathom
531	  haveI hrt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) rt :=
532	    isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) f
533	  haveI step1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) :=
534	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := rt) (e := eDf)
535	  haveI step2 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
536	      ((eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) ∘ₗ eTop.symm.toLinearMap) :=
537	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f)
538	      (f := eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) (e := eTop.symm)
539	  -- identify the target restriction map with `(eDf ∘ rt) ∘ eTop⁻¹`
540	  convert step2 using 1
541	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
542	  have hc := LinearMap.congr_fun hnathom (eTop.symm x)
543	  simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply] at hc ⊢
544	  refine (?_ : _ = _).trans hc.symm
545	  congr 1
546	  exact (eTop.apply_symm_apply x).symm
547	
548	/-- A morphism of sheaves of `R`-modules on `Spec R` that is an isomorphism on every basic open
549	`D(f)` is an isomorphism. This is the "isomorphism on a basis ⟹ isomorphism" reduction specialised
550	to the basic-open basis of `Spec R` (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`): injectivity on stalks is
551	`stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`, surjectivity on stalks is the basic-open germ lift, and
552	`isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` concludes. Project-local glue used to assemble `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`
553	from per-basic-open section data. -/
554	private theorem isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
555	    {F G : TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat.{u} R) (Spec R)} (α : F ⟶ G)
556	    (h : ∀ f : R, IsIso (α.1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) : IsIso α := by
557	  have hB : TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _)) :=
558	    PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
559	  have hinj : ∀ U ∈ Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _),
560	      Function.Injective (α.1.app (.op U)) := by
561	    rintro U ⟨f, rfl⟩
562	    exact ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).1
563	  have hstalk : ∀ x, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor (ModuleCat.{u} R) x).map α.1) := by
564	    intro x
565	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
566	    refine ⟨TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis hB hinj x, ?_⟩
567	    intro t
568	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, hUB, s, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist_of_isBasis hB G.presheaf x t
569	    obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := hUB
570	    obtain ⟨s', rfl⟩ := ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).2 s
571	    exact ⟨F.presheaf.germ _ x hxU s', by rw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]⟩
572	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso α
573	
574	/-- A linear map intertwining two localizations of the same module at the same submonoid is
575	bijective: if `f : M →ₗ M'` and `g : M →ₗ M''` both exhibit a localization at `S` and
576	`h : M' →ₗ M''` satisfies `h ∘ₗ f = g`, then `h` is bijective (it is the canonical localization
577	isomorphism `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`). Stated with the two `IsLocalizedModule` facts as
578	explicit hypotheses to avoid typeclass-diamond ambiguity at the call site. -/
579	private theorem bijective_comp_of_localizations {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
580	    {M M' M'' : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
581	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module R M''] {f : M →ₗ[R] M'} {g : M →ₗ[R] M''} {h : M' →ₗ[R] M''}
582	    (hf : IsLocalizedModule S f) (hg : IsLocalizedModule S g) (hh : h ∘ₗ f = g) :
583	    Function.Bijective h := by
584	  haveI := hf; haveI := hg
585	  have heq : h = (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).toLinearMap := by
586	    apply IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext S (f := f) (f' := g)
587	    apply LinearMap.ext
588	    intro x
589	    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, ← LinearMap.comp_apply, hh,
590	      LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv_apply]
591	  rw [heq]
592	  exact (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).bijective
593	
594	/-- **`IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` from per-basic-open section localization** (the cheap stalk/section
595	assembly of `lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the blueprint "G1-assemble" step). If for every
596	`f : R` the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` of a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R`
597	exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R` — exactly the conclusion of G1-core
598	(`lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`,
599	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`) — then the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is
600	an isomorphism (equivalently `M` lies in the essential image of `tilde`).
601	
602	On each basic open `D(f)` the component of `modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ` is a map between
603	two localizations of `N = Γ(M, ⊤)` at `powers f`: the source `Γ(tilde N, D(f))` localizes via the
604	instance `tilde.toOpen N (D f)` and the target `Γ(M, D(f))` localizes by hypothesis, and the two
605	localization maps are intertwined by `Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`. Hence the component is
606	the canonical localization isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`);
607	`isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen` upgrades this to an isomorphism of sheaves, and
608	`modulesSpecToSheaf` being fully faithful reflects it to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.
609	
610	This turns the remaining keystone obligation into exactly G1-core: combined with the converse engine
611	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the per-basic-open localization hypothesis is
612	*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. Project-local: Mathlib has no `IsQuasicoherent → IsIso
613	fromTildeΓ` bridge. -/
614	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
615	    (M : (Spec R).Modules)
616	    (H : ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
617	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
618	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom) :
619	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
620	  haveI hmain : IsIso (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ) := by
621	    apply isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen
622	    intro f
623	    set N := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤) with hN
624	    -- target localizes by hypothesis; source localizes by the `tilde.toOpen` instance
625	    haveI htgt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
626	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
627	          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := H f
628	    set comp := (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ).1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))
629	      with hcomp
630	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
631	    have hcompose := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
632	    -- the component intertwines the two localization maps of `N` at `powers f`
633	    have h1 : comp.hom ∘ₗ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom
634	        = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
635	          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
636	      have e := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hcompose
637	      rwa [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at e
638	    change Function.Bijective (⇑comp.hom)
639	    exact bijective_comp_of_localizations (Submonoid.powers f)
640	      (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule _ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom)
641	      (H f) h1
642	  exact SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.isIso_of_isIso_map M.fromTildeΓ
643	
644	/-- **Characterization of `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` by section localization.** For a sheaf of modules
645	`M` on `Spec R`, the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an isomorphism iff for every `f : R` the
646	section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
647	
648	The forward direction is the affine engine `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`; the
649	reverse is `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`. Combined with G1-core
650	(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`, not yet
651	formalized) — which supplies the right-hand side for any quasi-coherent `M` — this yields gap1
652	(`lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`). Project-local. -/
653	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
654	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) :
655	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
656	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
657	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom :=
658	  ⟨fun h f => by haveI := h; exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f,

662	
663	The keystone G1-core `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization` asks: for a *quasi-coherent*
664	`M : (Spec R).Modules` and `f : R`, the section restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is

669	presented* case, `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).

673	exactly the production of a global presentation/tilde from local (quasi-coherent) data on `Spec R`;
674	see the handoff in `task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/

676	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a globally presented module.** If `M : (Spec R).Modules`
677	admits a global `SheafOfModules.Presentation`, then for every `f : R` the section restriction
678	`Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
679	
680	This is the composition of Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (a global presentation
681	forces `M.fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism, i.e. `M` is a `tilde`) with the affine engine
682	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`. It is the Route-F endpoint for the
683	globally-presented case; the general quasi-coherent case additionally requires producing a global
684	presentation/tilde identification from the (Mathlib-absent) affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` bridge.
685	Project-local. -/
686	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
687	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (_P : M.Presentation) (f : R) :
688	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
689	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
690	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
691	  haveI : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation M _P
692	  exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f
693	
694	/-- **`map_units` field of G1-core (Route F), for any sheaf of modules.** For `M : (Spec R).Modules`
695	and `f : R`, every element of `Submonoid.powers f` acts invertibly on the sections `Γ(M, D(f))` over
696	`R`. This is exactly the first field `IsLocalizedModule.map_units` of the target
697	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, in the shape the 3-field constructor consumes.
698	
699	It holds for an *arbitrary* `M` (no quasi-coherence needed): on `D(f)` the element `f` already maps
700	to a unit of the structure ring `Γ(O_{Spec R}, D(f))` (the away-localization
701	`IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`), and the `R`-action on `Γ(M, D(f))` factors through it via
702	the scalar tower `R → Γ(O, D(f)) → Γ(M, D(f))`. Packaged from Mathlib's
703	`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen`. Project-local only as the *named* field
704	of the Route-F decomposition; the substance of G1-core is `surj`/`exists_of_eq` (see handoff). -/
705	theorem map_units_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R) :
706	    ∀ x : Submonoid.powers f, IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R
707	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj
708	        (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) (x : R)) := by
709	  rintro ⟨x, n, rfl⟩
710	  simpa using (tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen M f).pow n
711	
712	/-- **Finite basic-open cover refining a quasi-coherent presentation cover.** Given a
713	sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` together with quasi-coherent data `q` (a — possibly
714	infinite — open cover `q.X : q.I → (Spec R).Opens` of `⊤` with a presentation of
715	`M.over (q.X i)` on each member), there is a *finite* family of elements `t : Finset R`
716	whose basic opens cover `Spec R` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`), with each basic open `D(r)`
717	(`r ∈ t`) contained in some member `q.X i` of the presentation cover.
718	
719	This is the topological "finite-cover front" of `lem:exists_isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_cover`:
720	quasi-compactness of `Spec R` plus the basic-open basis (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`)
721	refine `q.X` to a finite basic-open subcover; the cover condition `q.coversTop` is read off the
722	`Opens.grothendieckTopology` sieve via `Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff`, and finiteness is extracted
723	through `Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`. To obtain `q` from `[M.IsQuasicoherent]`, take
724	`‹M.IsQuasicoherent›.nonempty_quasicoherentData.some`.
725	
726	The remaining (heavy) step toward gap1 — transporting each presentation `q.presentation i`
727	of `M.over (q.X i)` across `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` to `IsIso ((M|_{D(r)}).fromTildeΓ)` — is the
728	site-slice ↔ scheme-pullback transport, which has no Mathlib support at the pinned commit.
729	Project-local: Mathlib has no affine quasi-coherent → finite presentation cover lemma. -/
730	theorem exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
731	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
732	    ∃ t : Finset R, Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤ ∧
733	      ∀ r ∈ t, ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i := by
734	  classical
735	  set G : Set R := {r | ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i} with hG
736	  have hspanG : Ideal.span G = ⊤ := by
737	    rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff']
738	    rw [eq_top_iff]
739	    intro x _
740	    simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
741	    obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hxU⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ x (by trivial)
742	    rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff] at hf
743	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hUi⟩⟩ := hf
744	    have hxXi : x ∈ q.X i := (leOfHom hUi) hxU
745	    obtain ⟨V, ⟨r, rfl⟩, hxV, hVle⟩ :=
746	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens) hxXi
747	    exact ⟨r, ⟨i, hVle⟩, hxV⟩
748	  obtain ⟨t, htsub, htspan⟩ := (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite G).mp hspanG
749	  exact ⟨t, htspan, fun r hr => htsub hr⟩

751	end AlgebraicGeometry
