2959	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D)]
2960	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent}
2961	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}
2962	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
2963	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
2964	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
2965	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
2966	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent does NOT yet exist; it is the
2967	  % genuine new content of gap1 and is reused by gap2's section-localization characterization.
2968	  % isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation does NOT close it: that lemma needs a GLOBAL
2969	  % presentation of M, whereas here only per-D(r) tilde data is available.
2970	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S "Sections over principal opens",
2971	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2152-L2170).
2972	  % Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.3.
2973	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2974	  % "Let $X$ be a scheme. Let $f \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
2975	  %  Denote $X_f \subset X$ the open where $f$ is invertible, see
2976	  %  Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-f-open}.
2977	  %  If $X$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated, the canonical map
2978	  %  $$
2979	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{O}_X)
2980	  %  $$
2981	  %  is an isomorphism. Moreover, if $\mathcal{F}$ is a quasi-coherent
2982	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules the map
2983	  %  $$
2984	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})
2985	  %  $$
2986	  %  is an isomorphism."
2987	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
2988	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
2989	  Let \(M\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) (equivalently, by
2990	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} and
2991	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}, \(M\) is locally isomorphic to a
2992	  tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) on the members of a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) of
2993	  \(\Spec R\)). Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
2994	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localized module
2995	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); that is, it is
2996	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
2997	\end{lemma}
2998	% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
2999	% below is a project-internal descent (finite sheaf equalizer over the cover + flatness of
3000	% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is
3001	% gap1 itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.
3002	\begin{proof}
3003	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3004	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3005	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3006	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3007	  Refine the quasi-coherence cover to a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}_{j=1}^m\) of \(\Spec R\)
3008	  with each \(D(r_j) \le q.X_{i(j)}\)
3009	  (\cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). On each \(D(r_j)\) the restricted
3010	  counit is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), so
3011	  \(M|_{D(r_j)} \cong \widetilde{N_j}\) with \(N_j = \Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) a module over \(R_{r_j}\),
3012	  and the basic-open sections of \(M\) below \(D(r_j)\) are the corresponding module localizations
3013	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}).
3014	
3015	  Fix \(f \in R\). The sheaf condition for \(M\) over the finite cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) and its pairwise
3016	  intersections presents the global sections as the finite equalizer
3017	  \[
3018	    \Gamma(M, \top) \;=\; \mathrm{eq}\Bigl(\textstyle\prod_j \Gamma(M, D(r_j))
3019	      \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(M, D(r_jr_k))\Bigr),
3020	  \]
3021	  the Mayer--Vietoris / unique-gluing description of sections
3022	  (\cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}, \cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}); the same holds
3023	  with \(\top\) replaced by \(D(f)\) and the cover by \(\{D(fr_j)\}\). Localizing at
3024	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) is exact and commutes with this finite limit because localization is flat
3025	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}). On each cover piece the localization of
3026	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) away from \(f\) is \(\Gamma(M, D(fr_j)) = \Gamma(M|_{D(r_j)}, D(f))\), a
3027	  localization of the tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) computed by
3028	  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}; the same on the overlaps. Hence the localized equalizer
3029	  computes \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\), and the comparison map
3030	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is an isomorphism: the section

input.code:
import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Topology

namespace ProbeOver
variable {X : Type} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)

example : (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
  cover_lift := by
    intro Y S hS
    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
    intro x hx
    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
    -- V ≤ functor.obj Y = val⁻¹ Y.left
    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
    -- W := image of V in X
    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
      intro y hy
      obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy
      exact hVle hzV
    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
    -- sieve membership
    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
    -- goal: (S.functorPullback functor) (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle))
    show S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
    -- domain of this map is functor.obj (Over.mk ...) = val⁻¹ W
    have hdomle : (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
      intro z hz
      -- z ∈ val⁻¹ W means val z ∈ val '' V, val injective ⟹ z ∈ V
      have : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X) := hz
      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := this
      have : z' = z := Subtype.val_injective hz'eq
      exact this ▸ hz'V
    have := S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle)
    convert this using 1
    apply Subsingleton.elim

end ProbeOver

variable {X : Type} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)

theorem functor_cocont : (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
  cover_lift := by
    intro Y S hS
    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
    intro x hx
    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩

input.code:
import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Topology

namespace ProbeOver
variable {X : Type} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)

theorem inverse_cocont : (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
  cover_lift := by
    intro W S hS
    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
    intro y hy
    -- val y ∈ (inverse.obj W).left = val '' W
    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
    -- P ≤ (inverse.obj W).left = val '' W
    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
    -- V := preimage of P in ↥U
    set V : Opens ↥U := ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
      intro z hz
      have : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X) := hz
      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle this
      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
    show S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
    -- (inverse.obj V).left = val '' (val⁻¹ P) ≤ P
    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
      intro p hp
      obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp
      exact hp'V
    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
    · -- coherence for Over.homMk: (homOfLE hdomle) ≫ (Over.mk ...).hom = (inverse.obj V).hom
      apply Subsingleton.elim

end ProbeOver

import Mathlib

namespace ProbeOver
variable {X : Type} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)

-- assume the two cocontinuities as instances (proven above)
axiom fc : (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
axiom ic : (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)

noncomputable example (A : Type) [Category A] :
    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A := by
  haveI := fc U
  haveI := ic U
  haveI : (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
    Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _
  exact (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A

end ProbeOver

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence

The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
equivalence of *sheaf* categories
`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").

This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.

It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Topology

variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)

/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
  cover_lift := by
    intro Y S hS
    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
    intro x hx
    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
    have hdomle :
        (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
      intro z hz
      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := (hz : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X))
      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'V
    convert S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle) using 1

/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
Grothendieck topology of the open subspace `↥U` to the `U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck
topology. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
instance overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous :
    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
  cover_lift := by
    intro W S hS
    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
    intro y hy
    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
    set V : Opens ↥U :=
      ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
      intro z hz
      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle (hz : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X))
      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
      intro p hp; obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp; exact hp'V
    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
    · apply Subsingleton.elim

/-- The dense-subsite witness for the inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U`, assembled from the two
cover-lifting facts above. Project-local glue for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`. -/
instance overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite :
    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
  Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _

/-- **The over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence.** For a topological space `X`, an open
`U ⊆ X`, and any category `A`, the equivalence of sites `Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.

This is exactly the equivalence left as a TODO in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`. It is the
foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge (`lem:over_restrict_iso`,
`overRestrictIso`): once the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` is identified with the structure
sheaf of the open subscheme `U.toScheme` under this equivalence, a presentation of `M.over U`
transports (via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` + `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`) to a geometric
presentation of `M|_U`. Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
noncomputable def overEquivalence_sheafCongr (A : Type*) [Category A] :
    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
  (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A

end AlgebraicGeometry

end AlgebraicGeometry

variable {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C}

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma Adjunction.isCocontinuous_iff_coverPreserving (adj : F ⊣ G) :
    F.IsCocontinuous J K ↔ CoverPreserving K J G := by
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ⟨?_⟩, fun h ↦ ⟨?_⟩⟩
  · intro U S hS
    refine J.superset_covering ?_ <| h.cover_lift (K.pullback_stable (adj.counit.app _) hS)
    intro X f hf
    refine ⟨F.obj X, F.map f ≫ adj.counit.app _, adj.unit.app _, hf, by simp⟩
  · intro U S hS
    refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.pullback_stable (adj.unit.app U) <| h.cover_preserve hS)
    intro X f ⟨Y, g, u, hg, heq⟩
    suffices F.map f = (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm u ≫ g by
      simp [this, S.downward_closed hg]
    simp [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm, ← heq,
      Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm]

lemma Adjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous (adj : F ⊣ G) [F.IsCocontinuous J K] :
    G.IsContinuous K J := by
  have := adj.isRightAdjoint
  apply Functor.isContinuous_of_coverPreserving (compatiblePreservingOfFlat J G)
  rwa [← adj.isCocontinuous_iff_coverPreserving]

instance [F.IsCocontinuous J K] [F.IsLeftAdjoint] : F.rightAdjoint.IsContinuous K J :=
  (Adjunction.ofIsLeftAdjoint F).isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous J K

end

  - `AlgebraicGeometry.overEquivalence_sheafCongr` (noncomputable def, ~line 875)
- **Declarations blocked (0 added with sorry):** the gap1 cone targets `overRestrictIso` (C),
  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (P1), `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` (D),
  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent` (gap1) — NONE added (would require sorry; left absent).
- **sorry count across file:** 4 → 4 (the four protected stubs `hilbertPolynomial`,
  `QuotFunctor`, `Grassmannian`, `Grassmannian.representable` — out of scope). No new sorries.
- **Build:** `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme` GREEN; all 6 new decls
  `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## What was built and why
The iter-030 plan ordered the gap1 cone as **C → P1 → D → assemble**, with C
(`overRestrictIso : M.over U ≅ M.restrict U.ι`) flagged as the single slice-touching bridge. On
scouting, C decomposes into:
1. **(this session)** an equivalence of *sheaf* categories
   `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
   induced by `Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`;
2. identifying the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf
   `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under that equivalence (RingCat level);
3. lifting (1)+(2) to sheaves of *modules* via `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`;
4. relating the result to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι` (via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`).

Step 1 is **exactly the TODO left open in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`** ("show that both
functors of the equivalence `overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
I filled it. This is the foundational, purely topos-theoretic layer of C; steps 2–4 are the
geometric layer above it.

## overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous / overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous (lines 786, 815)
- **Approach:** direct cover-lift proof. A sieve covers in `(gT X).over U` iff its
  `Sieve.overEquiv` image covers in `gT X` (`GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff`); the
  `Opens.grothendieckTopology` covering condition is pointwise (`∀ x ∈ V, ∃ W f, S f ∧ x ∈ W`,
  `CategoryTheory.Sites.Spaces`). Transport a point/cover member across `Subtype.val : ↥U → X`
  (an injective open embedding): `U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen` for openness of the
  image, `Subtype.val_injective` + `Set.preimage_image`/`image_preimage` for the membership
  bookkeeping, and `Sieve.downward_closed` + `Subsingleton.elim` (Opens is a thin category) to
  match the produced arrow to the given sieve member. ~25 lines each.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite / overEquivalence_sheafCongr (lines 841, 875)
- **Approach:** `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` (needs both cocontinuities,
  now instances) → `Equivalence.sheafCongr`. One-liners on top of the cocontinuities.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous / overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous (lines ~848, ~862)
- **Approach:** `Adjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous` applied to the equivalence adjunctions
  (`e.toAdjunction : functor ⊣ inverse` and `e.symm.toAdjunction : inverse ⊣ functor`). For the
  functor direction the `e.symm.functor.IsCocontinuous` instance is supplied by an
  `inferInstanceAs` cast from the proved `inverse` cocontinuity. Added because
  `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` (step 3 of C) requires `IsContinuous` of both functors.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## overRestrictIso (C) — NOT ADDED (precise handoff to next iter)
- **Done (this session):** step 1 of the decomposition above (the sheaf-site equivalence,
  `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`, + the four continuity/cocontinuity/dense-subsite instances).
- **Remaining — step 2 (ring-sheaf identification), the next real obstacle:** produce
  `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U ≅ (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).inverse.obj
  U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` (or the analogous direct iso of `RingCat`-valued sheaves). Useful lead:
  `U.ι.opensFunctor = (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U` (the geometric restriction
  functor's opens action factors through the slice machinery), so the structure-sheaf comparison
  should be reducible to `Scheme.restrict` / open-immersion structure-sheaf lemmas
  (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.restrictFunctor` is `pushforward` along `U.ι.opensFunctor`,
  `Modules/Sheaf.lean:319`).
- **Remaining — step 3:** feed the ring iso of step 2 into
  `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:305`; the two
  `IsContinuous` instances it needs are now in-file) to get the sheaf-of-modules equivalence.
- **Remaining — step 4:** compose with `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
  (`Modules/Sheaf.lean`) to land `overRestrictIso : M.over U ≅ M.restrict U.ι` (note: the two
  sides live in different module categories, so the literal statement must be phrased *through*
  the step-3 equivalence functor — the blueprint `lem:over_restrict_iso` statement may need its
  Lean form sharpened to make this explicit).
- **Dead end avoided:** do NOT hand-roll the site equivalence from scratch — `Opens.overEquivalence`
  + the (now-proved) continuity instances are the right primitives. The `set_option
  backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` incantation the analogist flagged was NOT needed for
  the topological layer (the cocontinuity proofs go through plainly); it may still be needed for the
  module-level `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` synthesis in step 3.

## section_localization_descent (D) — NOT ADDED
- Gated on P1 (needs the per-`D(r)` local-tilde data, which needs C). Independently it is a genuine
  multi-step descent (finite sheaf equalizer over the cover + `IsLocalization.flat`,
  Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections` / Hartshorne II.5.3); see blueprint `lem:section_localization_descent`.
  Not attempted this session — C's foundational layer was the prioritized cheaper front.

## Why I stopped
**Real progress:** 6 axiom-clean declarations added (lines 786, 815, 841, ~848, ~862, ~875),
filling the explicit `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO that is the foundational
(topos-theoretic) layer of gap1's slice-to-geometric bridge C. Build green, all axiom-clean.

**Partial progress on the cone:** C is not complete. The precise next obstacle is the
**ring-sheaf identification** `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U ≅ (transport of) U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf`
(step 2 above) — a geometric lemma, not topos-theoretic. Stopped here rather than risk a long
geometric detour that cannot be left as a sorry. Informal agent unavailable (no LLM API key in env,
per PROGRESS.md standing note) — documented, not skipped.

## Needs blueprint entry
All 6 new decls are project-local infrastructure with no current blueprint block (they are
`lean_aux` nodes; `archon dag-query unmatched` will list them). They form the topological layer of
`lem:over_restrict_iso`. Suggested blueprint placement — a short sub-section under the gap1
slice-transport anchors (near `lem:over_restrict_iso`, blueprint L2875), one block per decl:
- `overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous` — cover-lifting of `Opens.overEquivalence U` functor;
  relies on `GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff`, `Opens.grothendieckTopology` pointwise covering,
  `Subtype.val` open-embedding injectivity, `Sieve.downward_closed`.
- `overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous` — cover-lifting of the inverse (same toolkit).
- `overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite` — `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous`
  on the two above.
- `overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous`, `overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous` —
  `Adjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous` on the equivalence adjunctions.
- `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` — `Equivalence.sheafCongr`; the sheaf-category equivalence
  `Sheaf ((gT X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (gT ↥U) A`. This is the keystone the C blueprint block
  (`lem:over_restrict_iso`) should `\uses{}`. Mathlib TODO filled.
